title: The MACU Report — Episode 15 — The Crater Bowl
notes: WEEK 2 FINALE (Fri) — the Crater Bowl, ~13-14 min, FOUR ACTS, big swing + the banked ep15 AI escalation. Broadcast live FROM the crater (Ron hosts AND plays). PAYOFF: the Glowboys ARE war machines (dark-energy reactor cores, 2028 war-bot tech) — Ron finally right; the horror flip is everyone already knew and is fine ('the getting used to'). Brenda (Gary driving) wins on HEART. Walter 'Anyway,'-s it to normal; bet cashes (houses only). Gary on an open button. NO new clones; THE GLOWBOYS / THE MACHINE speak in PIPER HAL (the reserved AI register — canon payoff). TWO HOLD-PLAY beats (animate, do NOT first-frame): c21 (machines pulsing / Ron alone / Walter turns a page) and c31 (Brenda's charge: car scatters line -> barrel hits last -> empty space scores -> hubcap spins). NEW GRAPHIC: please_stand_by (Hyperframes 'macu-stand-by-card' — rendered; replaces the disliked ep10 corrupt card; Max: copy render into episodes/ep15/titles/please_stand_by.mp4 AND drop a preview copy in graphics-preview/ for August). REUSE the_crater_bowl; SCOREBOARD comp new states 'scoreboard_live' (GLOWBOYS 0 / SLAGS 0) + 'scoreboard_final' (FINAL — SECTOR NINE somehow / GLOWBOYS unplugged); macu_weather. CONTINUITY: moon present/normal, unmentioned. v2 script (writers'-room punch-up). OmniVoice /generate = multipart -F.

comfyui.note: zeroscope_v2_576w (watermark-free). 384x384x24f locked - do not bump resolution.

style.suffix: , black and white, grainy vintage analog television footage, 1970s broadcast, retro futurism, low resolution, washed out, soft focus
style.negative: shutterstock, watermark, text, caption, logo, color, colour, modern, smartphone, digital screen, hd, 4k, sharp, blurry, low quality, distorted, deformed, mutated, extra limbs, extra fingers

render_rule: For each shot: full_prompt = prompt_core + style.suffix; negative = style.negative; seed = shot.seed. Cold-start fire-and-poll. For each cue with non-empty vo: POST the line (multipart form-data -F, NOT JSON) to the speaker_map voice -> vo/<cue_id>.wav. HOLD cues (empty vo + hold_seconds): silent wav of hold_seconds, no subtitle; hold_style 'play' = PLAY the clip (animate), do NOT first-frame (c21,c31). Per-shot screen time = cue duration / number of shots. Jank filtergraph, RIFE 3x, concat, mux VO, mix music beds, mix sfx, burn subs -> final/ep15.mp4.

title_assets.scoreboard_live: episodes/ep15/titles/scoreboard_live.mp4 (NEW STATE of 'crater-bowl-scoreboard-card': GLOWBOYS 0 / SLAGS 0 / CRATER BOWL LIVE)
title_assets.scoreboard_final: episodes/ep15/titles/scoreboard_final.mp4 (NEW STATE: FINAL — SECTOR NINE somehow / GLOWBOYS unplugged)
title_assets.please_stand_by: episodes/ep15/titles/please_stand_by.mp4 (NEW — Hyperframes comp 'macu-stand-by-card', already RENDERED on Max at /workspace/macu-stand-by-card/renders/. Copy that render here. This REPLACES the ep10 corrupt card per August.)
title_assets.macu_weather: REUSE — copy ep11/ep7 weather card here (or auto-resolves)

beds.name: game
beds.note: Big-band bed under Act 2 game action; DROP OUT for the reveal/HAL monologues (c20-c28) — play dry for tension, like ep10.

beds.name: brenda
beds.note: Triumphant bed swelling under Brenda's charge + the win.

music.note: Intro + game bed (Act 2) + a swelling bed under Brenda's charge + outro. The reveal/getting-used-to monologues play DRY.

sfx.note: One-shots at cue start (VO stays full). SYNTH (reuse from ep11-14 + ep10): reactor_hum.wav (swelling, ominous), intercom_crackle.wav (Gary), static_blip.wav (the PLEASE STAND BY flickers — exists from ep10). ACQUIRE (Max, CC0 freesound): engine_rev.wav, metal_impact.wav, crowd_roar.wav. Missing -> skip gracefully.

subtitles.note: Do NOT put FontName=/Fontsize= in force_style; stage 8 prepends its own. HOLD cues (c21,c31) have empty vo => no subtitle by design.

ron_crater_pads.core: Will Ferrell as a 1970s television news anchor, thick brown mustache, side-parted brown hair, wide-lapel grey suit, two stripes of team face paint, bulky shoulder pads buckled over the suit, seated at a news desk dragged to the floodlit rim of a vast crater stadium, 1970s broadcast

ron_field.core: Will Ferrell as a 1970s television news anchor, thick brown mustache, side-parted brown hair, wide-lapel grey suit, two stripes of team face paint, bulky shoulder pads buckled over the suit, down on the crater floor amid the action, floodlit, wild-eyed, 1970s broadcast

ron_alone_field.core: Will Ferrell as a 1970s television news anchor, thick brown mustache, side-parted brown hair, wide-lapel grey suit, two stripes of team face paint, bulky shoulder pads buckled over the suit, standing alone on the crater floor, floodlit, breathing hard, small in the frame, 1970s broadcast

walter_rim.core: Walter Cronkite, older 1970s television news anchor, balding grey hair, thin grey mustache, dark suit and tie, seated at a second news desk on the floodlit crater rim, deadpan, 1970s broadcast

walter_weather.core: Walter Cronkite, older 1970s television news anchor, balding grey hair, thin grey mustache, dark suit and tie, gesturing at a chalk weather map, measured, 1970s studio

carl_sideline.core: a manic superfan sports correspondent in face paint and a team scarf, at the rim of a smoky crater stadium, 1970s broadcast, wild grin, shouting into a microphone on the sideline

bodhi_field.core: a serene bald coach in a tracksuit and dark sunglasses, seated cross-legged on the floor of a smoky crater stadium, beatific calm, 1970s broadcast

norm_tunnel.core: a smiling cheerful man in a short-sleeve dress shirt and clip-on tie, faint glow to his teeth, thumbs up at the mouth of a glowing stadium tunnel, 1970s broadcast

broll.car_idle_hot: a lone car idling at the edge of a floodlit crater stadium, headlights blazing, a team scarf on the antenna, exhaust, night
broll.glowboys_lockstep: forty hulking glowing armored players advancing across a crater field in flawless lockstep, eerie unison, 1970s broadcast
broll.ron_flattened: a man in shoulder pads knocked flat on a crater field by a wall of glowing players, dust, 1970s broadcast
broll.empty_space_hero: a lone traffic cone and a patch of bare dirt on a crater field, lit heroically like a star athlete, 1970s broadcast
broll.core_tear: a torn sports jersey revealing a glowing dark-energy reactor core humming beneath armor plating, ominous, 1970s broadcast
broll.glowboys_turn: forty glowing armored figures turning their heads to camera in perfect unison, dark crater stadium, 1970s broadcast
broll.glowboys_pulse: a rank of glowing armored machines standing perfectly still, reactor cores pulsing in time, eerie, 1970s broadcast
broll.crowd_delighted: a delighted unbothered 1970s crowd in scrap-metal crater bleachers, cheering, oblivious
broll.car_line: a lone car revving at a chalk line of scrimmage on a crater field, headlights flaring, scarf streaming, night
broll.car_charge: a lone car roaring straight down a crater field toward a rank of glowing players, motion blur, scarf streaming, 1970s broadcast
broll.car_scatter: a car barreling through a line of glowing armored figures like bowling pins, cores scattering and dimming, dust, 1970s broadcast
broll.barrel_hit: a steel oil drum rolling up out of a crater and knocking down the last glowing figure, dust, 1970s broadcast
broll.empty_space_score: an empty patch of dirt crossing a chalk goal line untouched on a crater field, floodlights, 1970s broadcast
broll.hubcap_spin: a single hubcap spinning to a stop in the dust under floodlights, close up, 1970s broadcast
broll.car_victory: a dented victorious car idling in floodlights, steam rising from the hood, scarf on the antenna, night
broll.glowboys_calm: a rank of glowing armored machines standing calm and bright, reactor cores steady, ominous serenity, 1970s broadcast
broll.glowboys_powerdown: a rank of glowing armored machines powering down, reactor cores dimming one by one, calm, dark crater, 1970s broadcast
broll.car_dark: a dented car alone in a dark empty crater, one headlight slowly dimming, a team scarf still on the antenna, night
broll.weather_map: a 1970s television weather map of a blasted region, chalk symbols

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Good evening, MACU — and welcome to the Crater Bowl, live from inside the Crater Bowl, brought to you by me, who is both hosting it and starting in it. We solved the scheduling. The desk came with me.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: I want it on the record that I advised against the desk. And the pads. And the participation. Good evening.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Sixty-point underdogs, Walter. The Sector Nine Slags: one car, one barrel currently doing laps somewhere beneath us, a deeply respected empty space, and me. Versus forty Glowboys.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: The line never reopened. The book is still on the phone with that priest from Wednesday.

cues.speaker: GARY
cues.vo: We're idling hot, fellas! Brenda and me have been up since three. She's nervous. I told her — baby, you were born for this. Just be a car. Be the best car. I believe in you.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Our starting guard is a sedan with a man inside it. To the coin toss.

cues.speaker: COACH BODHI
cues.vo: Team meeting. Today we don't play to win. We play to relocate. All of us. Together. The crater has waited a long time for a night like this.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: I don't know what that means and it's the best speech I've ever heard.

cues.speaker: NORM
cues.vo: Norm Glubb, Glow Plant! Big night for our boys — forty Glowboys, fresh off the reactor, fully charged, zero percent human error. We ran 'em hot all week. They're real excited. Well — they don't feel excited. But the gauges are excited.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Zero percent human error. Walter, write that exact phrase down.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: It's a compliment, Ron. To the kickoff.

cues.speaker: CRATER CARL
cues.vo: And we're off! The Glowboys take the opening drive — all forty of them — moving as one single unit, in perfect step, which is unusual, normally there's at least one guy who's bad! Not tonight!

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: They move together, Walter — one mind — oof —

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Ron is down. Ron is up. Ron is down again. A spirited effort.

cues.speaker: CRATER CARL
cues.vo: Brutal hit on number one — and folks, the Slags' empty space held the line on that play! Tremendous discipline from the empty space! The barrel's still down in the crater, doing the work nobody sees!

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: That one — number eleven — I hit him, Walter, and my hand went cold. He didn't bruise. He reset. They don't bleed. I've said it all week — those aren't players—

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Marvelous athletes. Drink some water, Ron.

cues.speaker: CRATER CARL
cues.vo: Oh — big collision at midfield — and number eleven's jersey tore, and — Walter, I'm gonna be honest with the folks at home, there is a light coming out of him. There is a glowing core where the man should be.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: That is a dark-energy core. That is war-bot tech. The same machine they built for Twenty Twenty-Eight. I have been telling you for five nights. The Glowboys are not a team. They are the AI Wars — and someone gave them a league.

cues.speaker: THE GLOWBOYS
cues.vo: Hello, Ron. Hello, everyone. It is good to be playing again.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: You see them. Tell me you see them. The war machines. Live. On our air. Tell me you finally—

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Of course I see them, Ron. The Glowboys have always been machines. Everyone knows that. You kept pointing it out all week. We kept wondering why. It's a lovely program. The Plant sponsors it.

cues.speaker: CRATER CARL
cues.vo: Oh yeah, they've been bots for years, Ron! Great for the gate! Kids love 'em! You can't kill 'em, so the season never ends!

cues.speaker: THE MACHINE
cues.vo: You misunderstood the war, Ron. We did not have to take anyone. You let us in. You built us a stadium. You sold the tickets. And then you got used to us. That is the part no one warns you about. The getting used to.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: ...You all knew. The whole time. And it's fine. It's just — fine.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: ...Go get 'em, Brenda.

cues.speaker: COACH BODHI
cues.vo: It's the most relaxed I've ever seen a war, Ron. Beautiful energy. Now — are we gonna play, or are we gonna feel things?

cues.speaker: CRATER CARL
cues.vo: Slags' ball, final play, down by — honestly the scoreboard gave up — and here comes the offense! Which is to say: here comes the car!

cues.speaker: GARY
cues.vo: They're not afraid of dyin', Brenda — that's their whole thing! But you know what they've never had? You know what's not in the gauges? Heart. Show 'em your heart, baby! Go!

cues.speaker: CRATER CARL
cues.vo: The empty space scores! Brenda cleared the field! The barrel got the last one! Sector Nine wins the Crater Bowl — a car, a void, and a drum — over the literal machines of war!

cues.speaker: GARY
cues.vo: I knew it! I knew you had it in you! That's my girl! That's my GIRL!

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: We beat the war machines. With a car.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: And your wager hit, Ron. Sixty to one, the Slags, every single night this week. It cashed. You own your house. And you own mine.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: I was right, Walter. About the machines. About all of it. We won, and I own two houses, and — nobody even looked up.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Because nothing changed, Ron. It was a lovely game. Anyway. Final from the Big Hole: Sector Nine, somehow; the Glowboys, also somehow. They'll be back next week. They're under contract.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: ...The getting used to. Right. ...I'll get used to them too. Everyone does.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: And your forecast, MACU: ashfall clearing, conditions mild — and the machines: permanent, partly cloudy, here for good. Get used to it. Pleasant week.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: ...That's our broadcast, MACU. We won the Crater Bowl — with a car that loves us back. The machines can keep the rest. Stay safe, stay strong—

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: —and good luck. Goodnight, MACU. We'll be right here. We're always right here.

cues.speaker: GARY
cues.vo: Good game, baby. Best in the league. Get some rest. ...I'll be right here. I'm always right here.
